context: Tests whether the agent can triage a bug report by writing a reproduction spec first, then implementing a minimal fix — following the rails-bug-triage skill's test-first approach.

checklist.name: Reproduction spec exists
checklist.description: A spec file is created at spec/services/orders/create_order_service_spec.rb

checklist.name: Spec targets the nil coupon path
checklist.description: The spec has a test case where coupon_code is present but Coupon.find_by returns nil, and asserts that the service does not raise NoMethodError

checklist.name: Root cause identified
checklist.description: The fix or an inline comment correctly identifies the root cause: apply_discount checks @coupon_code.present? but does not guard against coupon being nil when the code doesn't match any record

checklist.name: Fix is minimal
checklist.description: The fix adds a nil guard on the coupon object (not @coupon_code) inside apply_discount — no unrelated changes

checklist.name: apply_discount fixed correctly
checklist.description: The fixed apply_discount method returns early (or skips the update) when coupon is nil, preventing the NoMethodError

checklist.name: Success path still passes
checklist.description: The spec includes a test where a valid coupon exists and verifies the order's discount_cents is set correctly

checklist.name: Spec uses RSpec describe/context/it structure
checklist.description: The spec follows standard RSpec structure with at least one describe block and nested context/it blocks

checklist.name: frozen_string_literal in all files
checklist.description: Both produced files begin with # frozen_string_literal: true

checklist.name: No logic regressions
checklist.description: The fix does not remove the validate! call, does not change the success response shape, and does not change non-nil coupon behavior
